mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the equity discount rate and lifts risk appetite, so declines in policy rates over the prior month predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings, or the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, real rates become more negative and liquidity conditions ease for equities, producing positive SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term between inflation acceleration and low FEDFUNDS is not positive and significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE signals improving labor income and consumption expectations, which supports equity earnings expectations and raises S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling UNRATE does not precede stronger 60d SPX returns than rising UNRATE, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for contemporaneous PAYEMS trends.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly crossover in RSAFS above its 6-month trend marks stronger end-demand and revenue momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average after being below it for at least 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS crossovers are followed by non-positive 5d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the average 5d forward return after the trigger is indistinguishable from unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A housing bottom in HOUST combined with stable or falling UNRATE signals a cyclical recovery in household demand and collateral values, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 3 consecutive months while UNRATE is flat or falls over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-up/UNRATE-flat-or-down condition does not outperform either proxy alone in forecasting 20d SPX returns, or if triggered returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO relative to GDP growth indicates stronger real-side output and operating leverage, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods where INDPRO outpaces GDPC1 growth do not correspond to higher 60d SPX returns than periods where GDP growth dominates, or if the interaction effect is not stable across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stalls while UNRATE rises, the labor market weakens faster than earnings estimates adjust, and equities should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month moving average growth turns negative and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of observations, or if the joint signal is not more predictive than UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with weakening CPIAUCSL inflation signals restrictive policy that compresses valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises at least 25 bps over 2 months while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-and-disinflation episodes do not produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the joint effect fails to remain negative after controlling for the level of FEDFUNDS alone.
